lesson.menu_title: Who Is Maria Clara? -- Diagnostic + Your First Words in English
lesson.menu_desc: Introducing yourself, without an interpreter -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 1 -- Who Is Maria Clara? -- Diagnostic + Personal Introduction
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 1 -- Diagnostic + Personal Introduction

listenings.text: Good morning, everyone, and welcome to our university. My name is Daniel. I am very happy to meet the group from Brazil. Today we visit three places. First, we visit the library. Then we have coffee together. At twelve o'clock, we have lunch in the main hall. Please stay with the group. If you have a question, you can ask me. Have a wonderful day.

Hello, Maria Clara. This is Grace from the university office. Welcome to China. I have two small things for you. First, your name badge is ready. You can take it at the front desk. Second, we have a welcome dinner on Tuesday, at seven o'clock. Please tell me if you can come. Thank you, and see you soon.

extra_audio.text: Hello! My name is Maria Clara. I am from Maceio. I work at a university. Nice to meet you.

items.situation: Someone smiles and says: "Hello! What is your name?"
tips: Hello! My name is Maria Clara.
I am from Maceio, in Brazil.
Yes, I have three children and one granddaughter.

items.situation: You don't understand the question. What do you say?
tips: Sorry, I don't understand. Could you repeat, please?
This is not a mistake -- it keeps the conversation open.
Could you speak more slowly, please?
Slower is your right, not a weakness.
